1.description: Sample time line. This figure illustrates the sample time line. Each tick on the horizontal time line represents a weekday.

2.description: Intraday quote frequency. This figure plots the average EUR/CHF quote frequency, measured as quotes per second, for each half hour in the day. The OTC quote frequency is based on quote updates by any of the eight selected dealers. The EBS quote frequency is based on order book updates that change the midquote. Time is expressed as Swiss local time and the labels correspond to the beginning of a period (e.g., 9 AM corresponds to 9:00 AM to 9:30 AM). The vertical dotted lines indicate the part of the day that is used in the main analysis: 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM. The figure is based on quote activity from January 19 to 23 and January 26 to 30, 2015. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

3.description: Network maps to illustrate the process of information revelation. The nine panels illustrate the process through which EUR/CHF information is revealed in eight dealer markets (DLR1-8) and an interdealer market (EBS). Each panel corresponds to a snapshot taken after the markets collectively experience a (multivariate) informational shock. The network vertices correspond to the various markets and the edges between them to the strength of a price response that is idiosyncratic to the pair (as measured by partial correlation in returns). A market's distance to the center is governed by itsPriceInefficiency, that is, by how much of the information in the shock the market has yet to reveal. The index τ indicates the time elapsed since the shock in increments of 100 milliseconds. The network plots are based on averages across estimates for each five-minute period in the baseline sample, defined as in TableI.

4.description: Scatter plot of dealer centrality versus price informativeness. This figure presents scatter plots of a dealer's centrality,Centrality0.2, versus two measures of his price informativeness. The first measure is a dealer's inefficiency half a second after a price shock,PriceInefficiency; see Panel A. The second measure is a dealer's information share at the time of a shock as captured by the lower bound of the Hasbrouck (1995) information share,InfoShare; see Panel B. The solid line is based on a simple linear regression and the shaded area depicts the associated 95% confidence interval. The reported variables are defined as in TableIIand averaged across eligible five-minute subsamples in the baseline sample, defined as in TableI. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

5.description: Scatter plot of dealer centrality versus quoted bid-ask spread. This figure presents a scatter plot of a dealer's centrality (Centrality0.2, defined as in TableII) versus his average bid-ask spread, defined as the duration-weighted average relative bid-ask spread. The reported variables are averaged across eligible five-minute subsamples in the baseline sample, defined as in TableI. The solid line is based on a simple linear regression and the shaded area depicts the associated 95% confidence interval. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

6.description: Information revelation on January 15, 2015: The EUR/CHF crash. This figure illustrates information revelation after the EUR/CHF crash on January 15, 2015, 10:30 AM. The various panels show how information was revealed across EBS and dealer markets (DLR 1-8) over the course of the day (dealers started to participate only after 11:30 AM). Each panel corresponds to a snapshot taken after the markets collectively experience a (multivariate) informational shock. The network vertices correspond to the various markets and the edges between them to the strength of a price response that is idiosyncratic to the pair (as measured by partial correlation in returns). A market's distance to the center is proportional to itsPriceInefficiency, defined as in TableII. All maps are based on price responses half a second after the shock (τ=5).

7.description: Characterization of trading and quoting activity on the day of the EUR/CHF crash. This figure presents the development of quote activity, prices, trading volume, and bid-ask spread on the day of the EUR/CHF crash. The OTC market metrics are based on quotes from all OTC dealers available, not only the eight dealers used in the main analysis. The EBS metrics are based on quotes that affect the best bid or ask price or the depth at those prices. Panel A shows quote activity, measured as the number of quotes in each minute. Panel B shows the EUR/CHF exchange rate on the left axis. The exchange rate for a given minute is defined as the median of quote midpoints posted in that minute. On the right axis, EBS buyer- and seller-initiated trading volume are plotted (in million EUR). Panel C shows the bid-ask spread in basis points, defined as the median of the log difference between all pairs of bid and ask prices. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]
